# Plan: Arena Chat Streaming Fix & UX Overhaul

## 1. Overview

The Arena chat experience is dysfunctional across multiple dimensions: no visible initialization state, broken streaming with no immediate feedback, no concurrency control (users can fire many messages with no response), no chunk ordering guarantees, and inconsistent behavior between VS Code and web clients. This plan fixes the streaming pipeline end-to-end, introduces a shared streaming manager in `packages/core/`, adds proper initialization and readiness UX, enforces single-stream concurrency, and cleans up redundant UI elements.

**Who it's for:** Wave Client users interacting with Arena AI agents in VS Code or the web app.

**Scope:**
- Shared streaming protocol layer in core (not duplicated per adapter)
- Ordered chunk delivery with sequence numbers
- Single-stream concurrency with input blocking
- Initialization & readiness UX
- Immediate feedback on send (connecting → streaming → complete)
- Remove duplicate references display; add context-window size circle indicator
- Cross-platform parity between VS Code and web adapters

**Out of scope:**
- New agent types or tools
- Ollama support in web adapter (separate plan)
- Vector store / web fetcher wiring
- Message history search or export

## 2. High-Level Features

- **F1: Shared Streaming Manager** — Extract streaming protocol (chunk ordering, reorder buffer, safety timeouts, heartbeat tracking, state machine) into a reusable hook in `packages/core/`. Both adapters produce raw `StreamHandle`s; the core hook wraps them with protocol guarantees. This is the architectural foundation — all other features build on it.
- **F2: Ordered Chunk Protocol** — Add `seq` (sequence number) to `ArenaChatStreamChunk`. `ArenaService` (backend) increments per-stream. The streaming manager maintains a reorder buffer that only appends content when `nextExpectedSeq` matches. Heartbeats and error chunks are exempt from sequencing.
- **F3: Send Guard & Concurrency Control** — Block input during an active stream. Only one stream per session at a time. If a previous stream is active, cancel it before starting a new one. The input bar shows a clear "Agent is responding…" state and a stop button.
- **F4: Chat Initialization & Readiness UX** — Replace boolean `arenaIsLoading` with a state machine: `'idle' | 'loading' | 'ready' | 'needs-config'`. Show appropriate UI for each state (skeleton/spinner during loading, configuration prompt when provider isn't set up, ready state for normal chat). Pre-send validation checks provider readiness before allowing message dispatch.
- **F5: Immediate Feedback & Streaming Lifecycle** — When the user sends a message, immediately show a "Connecting…" state on the assistant bubble (distinct from "streaming" bouncing dots). Heartbeats from the backend drive a visible progress indicator. Clear lifecycle: `idle → connecting → streaming → complete/error`.
- **F6: UI Cleanup — References & Context Size** — Remove the duplicate references button from `ArenaChatToolbar` (keep references only in the right pane). Replace the toolbar metadata section (messages/tokens/duration stats) with a single context-window circle indicator that transitions from blue (low usage) to red (high usage / approaching ~150K words), with increasing border thickness as it fills.
- **F7: Web Adapter Streaming Parity** — Align the web adapter's streaming with the shared streaming manager. The web adapter produces a raw `StreamHandle` (as it does today); the shared hook in core wraps it identically to the VS Code path. Add heartbeat emission and `seq` numbering to the web adapter's Gemini streaming path.

## 3. Tech Stack Additions

> The canonical tech stack is defined in `.spec-lite/memory.md` → Tech Stack.

No additions — see memory. All changes use existing dependencies (React, Zustand, Tailwind, LangGraph).

## 4. Data Model (High-Level)

> No new persistent entities. Changes affect in-memory types and runtime state only.

### Modified Types

- **`ArenaChatStreamChunk`** — Gains `seq?: number` field (sequence number, 0-indexed, per-stream). Heartbeat and error chunks may omit `seq`.
- **`ArenaView`** — No change (remains `'chat' | 'select-agent' | 'settings'`).
- **New type: `ArenaStreamState`** — `'idle' | 'connecting' | 'streaming' | 'complete' | 'error'`. Replaces the binary `arenaIsStreaming` boolean for the streaming lifecycle.
- **New type: `ArenaReadinessState`** — `'idle' | 'loading' | 'ready' | 'needs-config'`. Replaces the binary `arenaIsLoading` boolean for initialization.

### New Core Hook

- **`useArenaStreamManager`** — Wraps any adapter's `StreamHandle` with protocol guarantees:
  - Reorder buffer keyed by `seq`
  - Safety timeout (resets on chunk/heartbeat)
  - State machine transitions (`idle → connecting → streaming → complete/error`)
  - Single-stream enforcement (cancels previous handle on new stream)
  - Heartbeat tracking for UI progress

### Storage Strategy

No persistence changes — streaming state is ephemeral (in-memory Zustand + hook state only).

## 5. Interface Design

### 5.1 Updated `ArenaChatStreamChunk` Type

```typescript
interface ArenaChatStreamChunk {
  messageId: string;
  content: string;       // incremental delta
  done: boolean;
  seq?: number;          // NEW — 0-indexed sequence number per stream
  error?: string;
  heartbeat?: boolean;
  sources?: ArenaMessageSource[];
  tokenCount?: number;
  blocks?: ArenaChatBlock[];
}
```

### 5.2 Stream State Machine (Core Hook Output)

```
idle ──send()──▶ connecting ──firstChunk──▶ streaming ──done──▶ complete
                     │                        │                    │
                     └──error──▶ error ◀──error──┘               idle
                     │
                     └──cancel──▶ idle
```

### 5.3 `useArenaStreamManager` Hook API

```typescript
interface ArenaStreamManager {
  /** Current stream lifecycle state */
  streamState: ArenaStreamState;  // 'idle' | 'connecting' | 'streaming' | 'complete' | 'error'
  /** Accumulated streaming content for the active message */
  streamingContent: string;
  /** ID of the message being streamed to */
  streamingMessageId: string | null;
  /** Error message if streamState === 'error' */
  streamError: string | null;
  /** Start a new stream — wraps adapter.streamMessage() with protocol */
  startStream(request: ArenaChatRequest, assistantMessageId: string): void;
  /** Cancel the active stream */
  cancelStream(): void;
  /** Whether the input should be blocked (connecting or streaming) */
  isInputBlocked: boolean;
}
```

### 5.4 Readiness State (Initialization)

```
idle ──mount──▶ loading ──success──▶ ready
                   │                    │
                   └──no provider──▶ needs-config
```

The UI renders:
- **`loading`**: Skeleton placeholders / spinner with "Initializing Arena…"
- **`needs-config`**: Friendly prompt — "Configure a provider to get started" with a button to open Settings
- **`ready`**: Normal chat/agent-selection view

### 5.5 Toolbar Changes

**Before:**
```
[References (badge)] ─── [Streaming ⚡] ─── [Provider · Model ▼] ─── [Messages | Tokens | Duration]
```

**After:**
```
[Provider · Model ▼] ─── [Streaming ⚡] ─── [◉ context circle]
```

- References button removed from toolbar (lives in right pane only)
- Metadata stats (messages, tokens, duration) replaced by a single context-window circle
- Context circle: SVG ring that fills clockwise. Color interpolates from blue (`hsl(210, 80%, 55%)`) at 0% to red (`hsl(0, 80%, 55%)`) at 100%. Border/stroke width increases from 2px to 4px as usage grows. Tooltip shows "Session size: ~X words / 150K".
- Provider/model selector stays

### 5.6 Right Pane Changes

- References section: unchanged (this is the single place references live)
- Session metadata section: keep existing messages/tokens/duration display here (moved out of toolbar)
- Session list: unchanged

## 6. Security Considerations

> Standing security rules are defined in `.spec-lite/memory.md` → Security.

No plan-specific concerns — see memory. Sequence numbers are unsigned integers with no security implications. No new external API surface.

## 7. Architecture & Design (Plan-Specific)

### Plan-Specific Decisions

- **Streaming protocol in core, not adapters**: The key architectural change. Today, safety timeouts live in `vsCodeArenaAdapter`, heartbeat emission in `MessageHandler`, and chunk accumulation in `ArenaPane`. This scatters protocol concerns across 3 layers and 2 packages. The new design centralizes all protocol logic into `useArenaStreamManager` in `packages/core/`. Adapters become thin transport: they return raw `StreamHandle`s with no protocol awareness. This ensures identical behavior on both platforms without code duplication.

- **Reorder buffer with bounded capacity**: The reorder buffer holds at most 50 out-of-order chunks. If a gap persists for > 5 seconds, the buffer flushes in-order (best-effort). This prevents unbounded memory growth in pathological scenarios while still handling transient reordering.

- **State machine over booleans**: Replacing `arenaIsStreaming: boolean` + `arenaIsLoading: boolean` with typed string unions (`ArenaStreamState`, `ArenaReadinessState`) eliminates impossible state combinations (e.g., `isLoading && isStreaming`) and makes the UI logic exhaustive — every state has an explicit render path.

- **Context-window circle as word estimate**: Token counting varies by model/provider. Instead of trying to be exact, estimate session size as word count (`content.split(/\s+/).length`) and show progress against a 150K word budget. This is provider-agnostic and intuitively understandable.

- **`seq` is optional and best-effort**: The `seq` field on chunks is optional. If absent (e.g., from a provider that doesn't support it), the streaming manager falls back to arrival-order accumulation (current behavior). If present, the reorder buffer enforces ordering. This maintains backward compatibility during rollout.

## 9. Testing Strategy (Plan-Specific)

> Standing testing conventions are defined in `.spec-lite/memory.md` → Testing.

### Plan-Specific Test Requirements

- **`useArenaStreamManager` unit tests** — Test the full state machine: `idle → connecting → streaming → complete`, `idle → connecting → error`, cancel mid-stream, heartbeat resets timeout. Test reorder buffer with out-of-order chunks. Test single-stream enforcement (starting a new stream cancels the previous one).
- **Chunk ordering tests** — Deliver chunks with `seq` values out of order; verify accumulated content is correct. Deliver chunks with a gap > 5s; verify flush behavior.
- **Send guard tests** — Verify input is blocked when `streamState` is `connecting` or `streaming`. Verify input is unblocked on `complete`, `error`, or `cancel`.
- **Initialization state tests** — Test `loading → ready` transition. Test `loading → needs-config` when no provider is configured.
- **Context circle tests** — Verify color interpolation at 0%, 50%, 100%. Verify tooltip text.
- **Integration tests** — Render `ArenaPane` with mock adapter; send a message; verify streaming lifecycle end-to-end (connecting indicator → streaming dots → complete message).

## 10. Logging Strategy (Plan-Specific)

> Standing logging conventions are defined in `.spec-lite/memory.md` → Logging.

### Plan-Specific Logging Requirements

- **Stream lifecycle events**: Log state transitions (`connecting`, `streaming`, `complete`, `error`) with `sessionId` and `streamId` at `info` level.
- **Chunk reordering**: Log when a chunk is buffered (out-of-order) and when the buffer flushes, at `debug` level. Log with `seq`, `expectedSeq`, `bufferSize`.
- **Safety timeout**: Log when the safety timeout fires (no chunks received within threshold) at `warn` level with `sessionId`, `lastSeq`, `elapsed`.
- **Concurrent stream cancellation**: Log when a previous stream is force-cancelled due to a new send, at `info` level.

---

## Feature Dependency Order

```
F1 (Streaming Manager) ──▶ F2 (Ordered Chunks) ──▶ F3 (Send Guard)
         │                                                │
         └──────────────────────────────────▶ F5 (Immediate Feedback)
                                                          │
F4 (Initialization UX) ─────────────────────────────────────▶ F6 (UI Cleanup)
                                                          │
F1 ──▶ F7 (Web Adapter Parity)
```

- **F1** is the foundation — must be built first.
- **F2** and **F7** depend on F1 (they produce/consume `seq` via the manager).
- **F3** and **F5** depend on the state machine from F1.
- **F4** is independent of streaming but should be done before F6 (UI cleanup touches the same toolbar).
- **F6** is the final UI polish pass.
